channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senior SAPS officials and businessman granted R20,000 bail each in precious metals case
item.description: In a development that has drawn considerable attention across South Africa, Maj-Gen Feroz Khan, head of crime counter-intelligence, Maj-Gen Ebrahim Kadwa, the Gauteng head of the Hawks, and Durban bus...

item.title: NCCIA Summons Three Norwegians Over Defamation Complaint by Pakistani Businessman
item.description: LAHORE/LONDON: Pakistan’s National Cybercrime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has officially summoned three Norwegian nationals to Lahore for questioning in a defamation inquiry. The individuals named ar...

item.title: Warioba Urges National Reflection and Transparency After Election Violence
item.description: Former Prime Minister Joseph Sinde Warioba spoke about the urgent need for Tanzania to reflect on its current state and return to the principles that made the nation peaceful and united. In the interv...

item.title: Mbeki’s Warning on ANC Governance Ignored, Leading to Political Consequences
item.description: Jovial Rantao reflects on a crucial political warning from former President Thabo Mbeki to the ANC in March 2023, cautioning against prioritising factional interests over constitutional obligations. M...

item.title: Review of 'Sheep Detectives': A Whimsical Murder Mystery in the Barnyard
item.description: ABS-CBN’s review of the new film 'Sheep Detectives' highlights a unique combination of suspense and charm. The movie blends a classic murder mystery with a lighthearted barnyard setting, offering audi...

item.title: Lessons from Kosovo on Reintegrating ISIS Returnees for Australia
item.description: After the recent return of four women and nine children linked to ISIS from Syria, Australia faces the challenge of reintegrating them safely and effectively. While Australia has already assisted 31 r...

item.title: Pete Wicks reunites with Olivia Attwood at Heathrow following her Miami trip
item.description: Pete Wicks was spotted displaying a considerate gesture towards Olivia Attwood, picking her up from Heathrow Airport after her brand trip to Miami. The pair, who have been friends for nearly a decade,...

item.title: Felipe Staiti, Co-Founder and Guitarist of Enanitos Verdes, Dies at 64 After Health Complications
item.description: Felipe Staiti, the founding guitarist of the influential Argentine rock band Enanitos Verdes, has died at the age of 64 in Mendoza, Argentina, following ongoing health complications. His passing was c...

item.title: Passengers from cruise ship affected by hantavirus test positive
item.description: An American man and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship MV Hondius, which experienced a hantavirus outbreak, have tested positive for the virus, authorities confirmed. The French woman, amon...

item.title: The Early Artistic Journey of Salvador Dalí in the 1920s
item.description: Salvador Dalí, born in Figueres, Catalonia in 1904, was a Spanish surrealist artist celebrated for his exceptional technical skill and imaginative works. The 1920s were pivotal in Dalí’s artistic evol...

item.title: Observations of Distant Galaxy Clusters Confirm Newtonian Gravity on Large Scales
item.description: A recent study has tested Newton's law of universal gravitation on the largest scales ever observed, examining the motion of galaxy clusters billions of light-years away. Researchers measured the velo...

item.title: WestJet faces scrutiny over flight cancellations and denied passenger compensation
item.description: In March, dozens of WestJet passengers experienced cancellations that the airline attributed to safety-related maintenance. Among them were Brad Vanderwilk and his girlfriend, who were rerouted throug...

item.title: The Enduring Legacy of Pia Rossilli in 1950s and 1960s Fashion
item.description: Pia Rossilli, later known as Pia Kazan (1937–2020), was an American fashion model of Italian heritage whose striking beauty and sophisticated style defined mid-20th century fashion. She gained promine...

item.title: Legal group requests safeguards for Dela Rosa amid potential ICC proceedings
item.description: A Philippine law firm has filed a petition seeking protective measures for former Philippine National Police Chief, Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa, in connection with a possible rendition or legal proceeding...

item.title: Nigerian Military Says Airstrikes Targeted Only Bandits in Niger
item.description: On Monday, Nigeria’s defence headquarters officially denied media reports claiming civilian deaths from recent airstrikes in northern Niger state. According to Defence spokesperson Maj-Gen Michael Ono...

item.title: The Weeknd to conclude ‘After Hours Til Dawn’ world tour with Asian dates
item.description: The Weeknd has revealed the final leg of his highly successful ‘After Hours Til Dawn’ world stadium tour, which will feature a series of performances across Asia. Produced by Live Nation and sponsored...

item.title: Ottawa Police Officer Faces Assault, Harassment, and Firearm Charges Related to Alleged Intimate Partner Violence
item.description: An Ottawa Police Service (OPS) officer has been charged in connection with alleged intimate partner violence involving a civilian member of the police service. Kingston Police announced the charges in...

item.title: KMPDU Doctors Request Salary Review and Urgent Settlement of Arrears
item.description: Doctors under the Kenya Medical Practitioners, Pharmacists and Dentists Union (KMPDU) have formally requested a 55 percent salary increment and immediate clearance of pending arrears as part of the 20...

item.title: Eastern Cape man jailed for 20 years for murdering mother amid witchcraft accusations
item.description: A 47-year-old man from the Eastern Cape, Samuel Ndesi, has been sentenced to 28 years in prison for the 2025 murder of his 68-year-old mother, Retuie Ndesi, after accusing her of practising witchcraft...

item.title: Protest Against Rising Insecurity in Borno Postponed Due to Military Presence
item.description: On Monday, May 11, 2026, the Nigerian Army deployed heavy armoured vehicles and armed soldiers to Filling Polo, Maiduguri, where a protest was meant to hold over the worsening insecurity and abduction...
